cause_analysis.narrative: Stylez By Gary, LLC churned on 2026-02-11 after 329 days with Zoca. Classification: reactive fade — Zoca reached out 47 times, but the client only replied 6. A one-sided monologue in the weeks before churn. 1 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days — this is the clearest operational failure on Zoca's side.
recommendations: [Engagement] One-way outreach is a warning, not a save. Cap outreach at 5 attempts if no reply, then switch to a different channel or a different messenger (SP → AM → team lead) to break the pattern.
[SLA] 1 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days. Enforce a hard rule: every inbound gets a human response within 24h, every day of the week.
[SLA example] On 2026-02-07 via app_chat, client wrote: "Great Length’s Extensions…" — no outbound response in 30 days.

Hey Gary, Good Morning!

Because your direction clicks are high and you already accept walk-ins, people who come to your landing page are expecting phone calls so they can walk in directly. Or maybe they have some custom questions that you can call and solve for them, communicate with them and see whether you can accommodate any bookings immed…
I get more direction clicks because I don’t really have a website anymore. The landing page you created is not really Website worthy.

Follow ups actually matters Gary. They're not casually browsing on their couch, they're on Google Maps actively looking for a place to go. They're probably in their car, between errands, or trying to squeeze in a last-minute appointment. If you don't call them up, they're calling the next salon down the street.
